{
  "high_level_description": "A photoreal 1940s noir one-sheet of a haggard male painter in a shadow-striped studio, a glamorous femme fatale framed in the doorway behind him, rain streaking the window.",
  "background": "A cramped 1948 attic art studio at night, walls cluttered with half-finished portraits and stacked canvases fading into deep shadow. A tall rain-lashed window throws hard venetian-blind stripes across the floor and the back wall. The space recedes into smoky blackness with a single warm bulb glowing in the gloom.",
  "art_style": "shot on 35mm black-and-white film with selective warm amber tint, anamorphic lens, shallow depth of field, high-contrast chiaroscuro, heavy film grain, photorealistic, volumetric cigarette haze",
  "aesthetics": "classic film noir, rain-soaked melancholy, romantic tension, smoky, vintage 1940s",
  "lighting": "a single hard key light rakes across the painter from the side while venetian-blind shadows cut the frame, the femme fatale backlit and silhouetted in the doorway.",
